passions_detected.false_judgement: That virtue pursued without external recognition has less value
That self-perceived labels (delusional, fraud, lazy) represent truth about character
That flavour/taste pleasures provide genuine satisfaction

causal_observations.description: Assents to self-judgment labels (delusional, fraud, lazy, incompetent, frail) as accurate assessments rather than recognising them as passion-driven distortions.
Art pursuits abandoned because impulse depends on positive feedback — when feedback absent, impulse to create collapses.

value_observations.declared_as: if done through action, not verbalised, it is genuine growth
value_observations.observed_as: genuinely held principle — one of the strongest value insights in the journal
still seeking it unconsciously through desire for respect and acknowledgement

virtue_observations.evidence: Frank reflection that could challenge himself more. Recognises need to seek discomfort and adventure.
Practising critical thought with less cynicism. Beginning to hear both sides rather than just expose himself to them.
Acknowledging when could have been nicer. Seeking to catch injustice in the moment, not just reflect later.
Setting absolute rules to break bad habits. Starting a regime for consistency.

key_passages.summary: Vision of virtue through action not signalling — 'IF I do it through action, not verbalise it, then it is not signalling, it is done for my own growth, to seek happiness with nature'
Self-assessment: 'Delusional, fraud, lazy, incompetent, frail' — deep shame about creative pursuits and dependence on positive feedback
Eudaimonia vision: continual improvement, positive outlook, strong health, controlled emotions, released baggage, never missing the wonder of the moment
